edges.description: TP53 and RB1 alterations disrupt tumor-suppressor control of the cell cycle.
Genomically altered tumor cells co-occur with a small cell neuroendocrine phenotype.
Checkpoint loss permits expansion of malignant neuroendocrine tumor cells.
Neuroendocrine carcinoma phenotype co-occurs with clinically aggressive growth.
Proliferation supports local invasion through the bladder wall.

nodes.description: TP53 and RB1 pathway disruption weakens cell-cycle checkpoint control in small cell bladder carcinoma tumor cells.

evidence.reference_title: Small cell carcinoma of the urinary bladder: a clinicopathological and immunohistochemical analysis of 81 cases.
evidence.snippet: Inactivation of the RB1 gene may be implicated in the oncogenesis of bladder SmCC.

nodes.description: Malignant neuroendocrine tumor cells proliferate rapidly, contributing to aggressive local growth.

evidence.reference_title: Small cell carcinoma of the urinary bladder: a clinicopathological and immunohistochemical analysis of 81 cases.
evidence.snippet: bladder SmCC is an aggressive disease that is frequently present at an advanced stage.

nodes.description: Local invasion through the bladder wall produces advanced bladder cancer and increases the risk of nodal or distant spread.

evidence.reference_title: Small cell carcinoma of the urinary bladder: a clinicopathological and immunohistochemical analysis of 81 cases.
evidence.snippet: At presentation, over 90% bladder SmCC were at advanced stages with tumors invading the muscularis propria and beyond, and about 1/3 developed metastases to lymph nodes and other organs.

nodes.description: Tumor cells show small cell neuroendocrine differentiation in the bladder, with high-grade cytology and expression of neuroendocrine-lineage markers.

evidence.reference_title: Small cell carcinoma of the urinary bladder: a clinicopathological and immunohistochemical analysis of 81 cases.
evidence.snippet: Most SmCC expressed at least one neuroendocrine markers, including synaptophysin (73%, 41/56) (Fig. 2B), chromogranin (47%, 26/55) (Fig 3B), CD56 (95%, 39/41), and NSE (100%, 4/4).

nodes.description: Small cell bladder carcinoma tumor cells frequently carry TP53, TERT, and RB1 alterations, placing tumor-suppressor loss and telomerase activation upstream of cell-cycle disruption and aggressive tumor behavior.

evidence.reference_title: Comparative Genomic Characterization of Small Cell Carcinoma of the Bladder Compared With Urothelial Carcinoma and Small Cell Lung Carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: The most common genomic alterations in SCBC were in TP53 (87%), TERT (75%), and RB1 (70%).
